_instructions: Fill in fields below. Repeatable sections (parents, grandparents, siblings, children, pets) are arrays — add as many entries as needed. Load via: lv80PreloadNarrator(template) or lv80PreloadFromFile(file)
_trainerPrompt: Use this trainer to show concise, anchored answers with time, place, and people.

parents.notableLifeEvents: Nurtured William's early interests in the arts.
Operated a clothing business in Montreal; son of Jewish immigrants.
parents.notes: Original family name was Schattner before being anglicized.

grandparents.culturalBackground: Immigrant from the Bukovina region (Austro-Hungarian Empire).
grandparents.memorableStories: He was the one who anglicized the family name from 'Schattner' to 'Shatner' upon moving to Canada.

spouse.narrative: Married in 2001; divorced in 2020 but remained close. They share a deep passion for the American Saddlebred industry.

children.narrative: Eldest daughter; worked as an Occupational Therapist.
Middle daughter; worked as a writer and actress.
Youngest daughter; pursued acting in the 1990s.

marriage.weddingDetails: Four marriages: Gloria Rand (1956–1969), Marcy Lafferty (1973–1996), Nerine Kidd (1997–1999), and Elizabeth Martin (2001–2020).

familyTraditions.description: E-biking excursions to stay active with younger family members.

earlyMemories.firstMemory: The physical geography of Montreal and the Jewish community in Notre-Dame-de-Grace.
earlyMemories.significantEvent: Joining the Montreal Children's Theatre, which set the stage for his lifelong acting career.

education.schooling: West Hill High School; Willingdon Elementary School.
education.higherEducation: Bachelor of Commerce from McGill University (1952).
education.earlyCareer: Business manager for a Montreal theatre; then moved to Ottawa's Canadian National Repertory Theatre.
education.careerProgression: Shakespearean actor at Stratford Festival; Broadway debut in 'The Tamburlaine the Great'; Cast as Captain Kirk in 1966.
education.communityInvolvement: Extensive work with therapeutic riding organizations and environmental causes.

laterYears.retirement: Firmly refuses to retire, stating 'Say yes to life' is the secret to healthy aging.
laterYears.lifeLessons: 'I know that good, healthy aging comes from not being solitary or brooding'.
laterYears.adviceForFutureGenerations: 'Say yes to everything new, because time is too short to lose an opportunity'.

hobbies.hobbies: E-biking, breeding horses, competitive reining, and digital photography.
hobbies.worldEvents: Becoming the oldest person to travel to space via Blue Origin in 2021.
hobbies.personalChallenges: Overcoming the sudden death of his third wife, Nerine, and navigating life in the public eye for over 70 years.
hobbies.travel: Global conventions; filming in locations from Vancouver to Japan.

health.healthMilestones: Suffered from Tinnitus for decades; successfully managed through therapy.
health.lifestyleChanges: Incorporating electric bicycles to keep pace with his grandchildren.
health.wellnessTips: Mental agility is maintained through creativity and imaginative compensation for physical decline.

technology.firstTechExperience: Radio broadcasts during his youth in Montreal.
technology.culturalPractices: Raised in Conservative Judaism; later focused on secular spirituality and environmentalism.

additionalNotes.unfinishedDreams: Exploring the 'Overview Effect' and its impact on human consciousness after his space flight.
additionalNotes.messagesForFutureGenerations: 'There is nothing better for your brain than trying to help someone else'.
